text:
The Amendment to the Constitution that brought in Prohibition in 1920 was the **18th Amendment**.

The 18th Amendment, which prohibited the manufacture, sale, and transportation of intoxicating liquors, was ratified by the states on January 16, 1919. Crucially, the amendment itself stated that it would go into effect "one year from the ratification." Therefore, counting one year from January 16, 1919, leads to January 17, 1920, the date when Prohibition officially began.

FINAL ANSWER: The 18th Amendment
